name: ValidateCommand
purpose: Check that the command is one of the allowed values and that required fields in message_data are present for that command.
behavior: If command is not in ['send', 'history', 'create_channel', 'join'], return error. For each command, verify required fields exist in message_data (e.g., channel_id for send/history/join, channel_name for create_channel, user_id for all).

semantic_inputs.name: message_data
semantic_inputs.description: Payload with fields like content, channel_id, user_id, channel_name

semantic_outputs.name: validation_result
semantic_outputs.description: Object with success flag and error message if invalid

postconditions: If invalid, parent returns error immediately
guarantees: Only valid commands and required fields proceed
stop_reason: Pure validation function with no further decomposition needed.

inputs.name: message_data
inputs.description: Payload with fields like content, channel_id, user_id, channel_name

outputs.name: validation_result
outputs.description: Object with success flag and error message if invalid

signature: def ValidateCommand(command: str, message_data: dict) -> dict

constraints.description: Must not access any data store; pure validation only.

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns error for missing required fields.
Returns success for valid command with all required fields.

name: ExecuteSend
purpose: Handle the 'send' command: validate membership, store message, update user's last_seen, notify channel members.
behavior: Check if user is member of channel via channels data. If not, return error. If yes, generate message_id, store message in messages store, update user's last_seen in users store, and prepare notification data for other members.

semantic_outputs.name: send_result
semantic_outputs.description: Object with success flag, data (message_id), and message

stop_reason: Can be further decomposed into membership check, store, update, notify.

outputs.name: send_result
outputs.description: Object with success flag, data (message_id), and message

signature: def ExecuteSend(message_data: dict, channels: dict, messages: dict, users: dict) -> dict

global_vars.description: Write new message entry; read for notification preparation
Read user data for last_seen update

data_operations.description: Write new message entry; read for notification preparation
Read user data for last_seen update

constraints.description: Must check channel membership before writing message.

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns error if user is not a member of the channel.
Successfully stores message and updates last_seen.

name: ExecuteHistory
purpose: Handle the 'history' command: retrieve last 100 messages from a channel sorted by timestamp descending.
behavior: Query messages store for all messages with given channel_id, sort by timestamp descending, limit to 100, return list.

semantic_outputs.name: history_result
semantic_outputs.description: Object with success flag, data (list of messages), and message

stop_reason: Pure query operation with no further decomposition needed.

outputs.name: history_result
outputs.description: Object with success flag, data (list of messages), and message

signature: def ExecuteHistory(message_data: dict, messages: dict) -> dict

global_vars.description: Read messages for the given channel

data_operations.description: Read messages for the given channel

constraints.description: Must not modify any data store.

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns at most 100 messages sorted by timestamp descending.
Returns empty list if channel has no messages.

name: ExecuteCreateChannel
purpose: Handle the 'create_channel' command: create new channel with unique name, set caller as creator and first member.
behavior: Check if channel name already exists in channels store. If yes, return error. If no, generate channel_id, create channel entry with members list containing user_id, store in channels.

semantic_outputs.name: create_channel_result
semantic_outputs.description: Object with success flag, data (channel_id), and message

stop_reason: Atomic operation with uniqueness check and creation.

outputs.name: create_channel_result
outputs.description: Object with success flag, data (channel_id), and message

signature: def ExecuteCreateChannel(message_data: dict, channels: dict) -> dict

global_vars.description: Read for uniqueness check; write new channel entry

data_operations.description: Read for uniqueness check; write new channel entry

constraints.description: Must ensure channel name is unique before creating.

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns error if channel name already exists.
Successfully creates channel with creator as first member.

name: ExecuteJoin
purpose: Handle the 'join' command: add user to channel's member list if not already a member.
behavior: Check if user_id is already in channel's members list. If yes, return error. If no, add user_id to members list and update channels store.

semantic_outputs.name: join_result
semantic_outputs.description: Object with success flag, data (updated member list), and message

stop_reason: Simple membership update with duplicate check.

outputs.name: join_result
outputs.description: Object with success flag, data (updated member list), and message

signature: def ExecuteJoin(message_data: dict, channels: dict) -> dict

global_vars.description: Read for membership check; write updated member list

data_operations.description: Read for membership check; write updated member list

constraints.description: Must check if user is already a member before adding.

acceptance_criteria.description: Returns error if user is already a member.
Successfully adds user to channel member list.
